ogTitle: Royal George vs Rushy Lagoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Royal George and Rushy Lagoon.
heading: Royal George vs Rushy Lagoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Royal George, Tasmania and Rushy Lagoon, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rushy Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Royal George (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Royal George or Rushy Lagoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Royal George has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Rushy Lagoon scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Royal George scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Rushy Lagoon scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Royal George or Rushy Lagoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rushy Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
